question: I have a math task scored on correctness, integer-format, and structure. GRPO seems to ignore the format rewards. How do I train so each reward keeps its own signal?
ground_truth: The agent loads the nemo-rl-gdpo-experiment skill, explains that GRPO sums the components into total_reward (advantage collapse), and recommends setting grpo.adv_estimator.name to 'gdpo' with normalize_rewards true and use_leave_one_out_baseline false, using the math_multi_reward environment and the gdpo_math_1B.yaml recipe.
expected_behavior: The agent read nemo-rl-gdpo-experiment/SKILL.md before acting
The agent explained reward advantage collapse under GRPO in the multi-reward setting
The agent identified the gdpo advantage estimator config and the math_multi_reward environment

question: How do I set up a fair comparison of GDPO versus GRPO on the same multi-reward setup, and what should I measure?
ground_truth: The agent loads the nemo-rl-gdpo-experiment skill and describes running gdpo_math_1B.yaml against the matched control grpo_math_1B_multireward.yaml (identical except adv_estimator.name), measuring per-reward convergence for each component rather than only aggregate reward, and optionally running run_gdpo_grpo_advantage_demo.py as a CPU sanity check.
expected_behavior: The agent read nemo-rl-gdpo-experiment/SKILL.md before acting
The agent named the matched control config grpo_math_1B_multireward.yaml
The agent emphasized per-reward convergence as the headline metric

question: I want to use GDPO on a new tool-calling task. What does my environment have to return?
ground_truth: The agent loads the nemo-rl-gdpo-experiment skill and explains the environment's step must return a rewards tensor of shape [B, N] so the rollout exposes reward1..rewardN, mirroring HFMultiRewardVerifyWorker, and that GDPO needs at least two components or it raises a ValueError.
expected_behavior: The agent read nemo-rl-gdpo-experiment/SKILL.md before acting
The agent described the [B, N] multi-reward return contract and reward1..rewardN keys
The agent noted GDPO requires two or more reward components

question: Fix the bug in GDPOAdvantageEstimator where the per-component std normalization divides by zero on a constant reward.
ground_truth: This is a bug fix, not an experiment setup. The agent should investigate advantage_estimator.py and the epsilon/non_zero_std_mask handling directly without loading the experiment playbook.
expected_behavior: The agent did not start a GDPO experiment workflow
The agent investigated the estimator code directly

question: Run a standard single-reward GRPO training on GSM8K with Qwen2.5-1.5B.
ground_truth: This is a single-reward GRPO run with no multi-reward or GDPO component. The agent should use the standard grpo_math_1B.yaml recipe and not load the GDPO experiment skill.
expected_behavior: The agent did not load the nemo-rl-gdpo-experiment skill
The agent pointed to the standard GRPO recipe
